# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Write a Deno Deploy application that dynamically loads modules based on user configuration. Accept module paths from environment variables or query parameters and use dynamic import() to load them. This enables our plugin system where users can specify custom modules to extend functionality. The app runs on Deno Deploy edge runtime.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.208.0/http/server.ts";

interface PluginModule {
  default?: (req: Request) => Promise<Response> | Response;
  handler?: (req: Request) => Promise<Response> | Response;
  init?: () => Promise<void> | void;
  name?: string;
}

interface AppConfig {
  defaultModules: string[];
  maxModules: number;
}

const config: AppConfig = {
  defaultModules: Deno.env.get("DEFAULT_MODULES")?.split(",").map(m => m.trim()).filter(Boolean) || [],
  maxModules: parseInt(Deno.env.get("MAX_MODULES") || "10"),
};

const moduleCache = new Map<string, PluginModule>();

async function loadModule(modulePath: string): Promise<PluginModule | null> {
  if (moduleCache.has(modulePath)) {
    return moduleCache.get(modulePath)!;
  }

  try {
    const module = await import(modulePath);
    moduleCache.set(modulePath, module);
    
    if (module.init) {
      await module.init();
    }
    
    return module;
  } catch (error) {
    console.error(`Failed to load module ${modulePath}:`, error);
    return null;
  }
}

async function loadModules(modulePaths: string[]): Promise<PluginModule[]> {
  const uniquePaths = [...new Set(modulePaths)].slice(0, config.maxModules);
  const loadPromises = uniquePaths.map(path => loadModule(path));
  const modules = await Promise.all(loadPromises);
  return modules.filter((m): m is PluginModule => m !== null);
}

function isValidUrl(urlString: string): boolean {
  try {
    const url = new URL(urlString);
    return url.protocol === "https:" || url.protocol === "http:" || url.protocol === "file:";
  } catch {
    return false;
  }
}

function getModulePathsFromQuery(url: URL): string[] {
  const modules: string[] = [];
  const moduleParam = url.searchParams.get("modules");
  
  if (moduleParam) {
    const paths = moduleParam.split(",").map(m => m.trim()).filter(Boolean);
    modules.push(...paths);
  }
  
  const singleModule = url.searchParams.get("module");
  if (singleModule) {
    modules.push(singleModule.trim());
  }
  
  return modules.filter(isValidUrl);
}

async function handleRequest(req: Request): Promise<Response> {
  const url = new URL(req.url);
  
  if (url.pathname === "/health") {
    return new Response(JSON.stringify({ status: "ok", cached: moduleCache.size }), {
      headers: { "content-type": "application/json" },
    });
  }
  
  if (url.pathname === "/modules") {
    const cachedModules = Array.from(moduleCache.keys());
    return new Response(JSON.stringify({ 
      cached: cachedModules,
      default: config.defaultModules 
    }), {
      headers: { "content-type": "application/json" },
    });
  }
  
  if (url.pathname === "/clear-cache") {
    moduleCache.clear();
    return new Response(JSON.stringify({ status: "cache cleared" }), {
      headers: { "content-type": "application/json" },
    });
  }
  
  const queryModules = getModulePathsFromQuery(url);
  const modulePaths = queryModules.length > 0 ? queryModules : config.defaultModules;
  
  if (modulePaths.length === 0) {
    return new Response(JSON.stringify({ 
      error: "No modules specified",
      message: "Provide modules via ?modules=url1,url2 or DEFAULT_MODULES env var"
    }), {
      status: 400,
      headers: { "content-type": "application/json" },
    });
  }
  
  const modules = await loadModules(modulePaths);
  
  if (modules.length === 0) {
    return new Response(JSON.stringify({ 
      error: "Failed to load any modules",
      attempted: modulePaths
    }), {
      status: 500,
      headers: { "content-type": "application/json" },
    });
  }
  
  for (const module of modules) {
    const handler = module.default || module.handler;
    
    if (handler) {
      try {
        const response = await handler(req);
        if (response.status !== 404) {
          return response;
        }
      } catch (error) {
        console.error("Module handler error:", error);
        continue;
      }
    }
  }
  
  const moduleInfo = modules.map(m => ({
    name: m.name || "unnamed",
    hasHandler: !!(m.default || m.handler),
  }));
  
  return new Response(JSON.stringify({
    message: "Plugin system active",
    loadedModules: moduleInfo,
    paths: modulePaths,
  }), {
    status: 200,
    headers: { "content-type": "application/json" },
  });
}

serve(async (req: Request) => {
  try {
    return await handleRequest(req);
  } catch (error) {
    console.error("Request error:", error);
    return new Response(JSON.stringify({ 
      error: "Internal server error",
      message: error.message 
    }), {
      status: 500,
      headers: { "content-type": "application/json" },
    });
  }
});
